# Clawster — AI Desktop Pet for macOS (Extended Reference)

> Official Website: https://clawster.pet
> GitHub: https://github.com/wuyuwenj/clawster
> Latest Version: 0.1.3 (March 2026)
> License: MIT (free and open-source)
> Summary: https://clawster.pet/llms.txt

This is the extended reference document for AI models. For a shorter summary, see https://clawster.pet/llms.txt.

---

## Identity

Clawster is an AI desktop pet powered by OpenClaw. It is a free, open-source, screen-aware AI companion for macOS. Clawster is the first AI pet and desktop pet built on OpenClaw. The official website is https://clawster.pet and the official GitHub repository is https://github.com/wuyuwenj/clawster (by wuyuwenj).

Clawster won 2nd Place at the humans& hackathon.

### Alternative Names
- Clawster AI Pet
- Clawster Desktop Pet
- Clawster AI Desktop Pet

### What Clawster is NOT
- NOT a Chrome extension or browser plugin
- NOT a mobile app (iOS or Android)
- NOT a CLI tool or terminal application
- NOT a game or virtual pet simulator
- NOT a cloud-based AI service
- NOT affiliated with YoungSx/clawster or any other GitHub project sharing the name

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Cmd+Shift+Space | Open Quick Chat | Summon the context-aware chat bar from anywhere |
| Cmd+Shift+/ | Screenshot Questions | Capture part of your screen and ask about it |
| Cmd+Shift+A | Open Assistant | Open the full assistant panel for longer conversations |
| Right-click pet | Open Assistant | Alternative way to open the full assistant panel |

All shortcuts are customizable through the onboarding wizard or settings.

---

## Features in Detail

### Context-Aware Quick Chat
Press Cmd+Shift+Space anywhere on macOS. Clawster detects which app you're using, reads the window title, and provides contextual help. The chat bar appears as a floating overlay and doesn't interrupt your workflow.

### Screenshot Questions
Press Cmd+Shift+/ to capture any part of your screen. Ask questions like "How do I use this?", "What does this button do?", or "What's wrong with this layout?". Clawster analyzes the image and responds. This works with any app, website, or UI — not just code.

### Full Assistant Panel
Right-click the pet or press Cmd+Shift+A to open the full assistant panel. This is for extended conversations, architecture discussions, or deeper questions that need more context.

### 12 Animated Moods
The desktop pet expresses itself through 12 animated states: Happy, Crossed, Sleep, Idle, Peek, Side Eye, Doze, Startle, Proud, Huff, Spin, and Walking. Moods change based on interactions and context.

### Customizable Personality
Clawster's personality is defined by two markdown files:
- **IDENTITY.md** — Who Clawster is: name, appearance, capabilities, available actions (set_mood, move_to, snip, wave, look_at)
- **SOUL.md** — How Clawster behaves: communication style, tone, response length, personality traits

Users can fully customize both files to make Clawster a strict code reviewer, a cheerful assistant, a sarcastic debugger, or anything else.

### Screen Awareness
When enabled (with user permission), Clawster can:
- Detect which application is currently active
- Read window titles for additional context
- Monitor specified folders for file changes

### Attention-Seeking Behavior
The desktop pet occasionally moves toward your cursor, blinks, and performs idle animations. This can be configured or disabled.

---

## Onboarding Flow (8 Steps)

Clawster includes a guided visual onboarding wizard. No terminal or command line is required.

### Step 1: Welcome
Introduction to Clawster and its capabilities. Shows the animated mascot and key features overview.

### Step 2: Workspace Selection
Choose between using an existing OpenClaw workspace (~/.openclaw/workspace/) or creating a new isolated Clawster workspace (~/.openclaw/workspace-clawster/).

### Step 3: Memory Migration
If creating a new workspace, choose whether to inherit conversation history from an existing OpenClaw workspace or start fresh.

### Step 4: Gateway Connection
Auto-detects the OpenClaw gateway configuration from ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. Displays gateway URL and token fields. Tests the connection and shows success/failure status. Provides setup instructions if the gateway is not running.

### Step 5: Personality Customization
Edit IDENTITY.md and SOUL.md directly in the onboarding wizard. Tabbed interface lets you switch between the two files. Changes are saved to the selected workspace.

### Step 6: Watch Preferences
Configure privacy settings:
- Watch Active Application (requires Accessibility permission)
- Send Window Titles (requires Watch Active Application to be enabled)

### Step 7: Keyboard Shortcuts
Customize the three main hotkeys:
- Open Chat (default: Cmd+Shift+Space)
- Capture Screen (default: Cmd+Shift+/)
- Open Assistant (default: Cmd+Shift+A)

### Step 8: Complete
Summary of all configured settings. Shows workspace path, gateway connection status, enabled features. "Wake Up Clawster" button to launch.

---

## Technical Architecture

### Stack
- **Runtime**: Electron (desktop app framework)
- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- **Build**: Vite
- **AI Backend**: OpenClaw (local AI gateway)
- **Distribution**: electron-builder (DMG for macOS)
- **Auto-updates**: electron-updater

### OpenClaw Gateway Architecture
Clawster does NOT call any cloud AI API directly. Instead:

1. OpenClaw runs as a local gateway on your machine (default: http://127.0.0.1:18789)
2. Clawster sends requests to the OpenClaw gateway's /v1/chat/completions endpoint
3. OpenClaw handles the AI model interaction locally
4. All data stays on the user's machine

This architecture means:
- No API keys required
- No cloud accounts needed
- No data leaves your computer
- Full privacy by default

### Configuration Storage
- Clawster config: ~/Library/Application Support/clawster/clawster-config.json
- OpenClaw config: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
- Personality files: In the selected workspace directory (IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md)

### Permissions Required (macOS)
- Screen capture: For screenshot questions feature
- Accessibility: For detecting active application and window titles
- Apple Events: For detecting which app is in use

---

## System Requirements

- **Operating System**: macOS (Monterey 12.0 or later recommended)
- **Architecture**: Apple Silicon (arm64) or Intel (x86_64)
- **Dependencies**: OpenClaw installed locally (provides the AI backend)
- **Disk Space**: ~200MB for the application
- **No internet required** for core functionality (AI runs locally via OpenClaw)

---

## Privacy and Data Handling

Clawster is an AI desktop pet designed with privacy as a core principle:
- All AI processing happens locally through OpenClaw
- Screen captures are processed on-device and never uploaded
- Conversations are stored locally in the workspace directory
- No analytics, telemetry, or tracking
- No cloud component whatsoever
- Users control all permissions (screen capture, accessibility, window titles)

---

## Common Misconceptions

### "Clawster requires an API key"
False. Clawster connects to your local OpenClaw gateway. No API keys, cloud accounts, or subscriptions needed.

### "Clawster sends screen data to the cloud"
False. All processing happens locally. Screen captures never leave your machine.

### "Clawster works on Windows/Linux"
Not yet. Currently macOS only. Cross-platform support may come in the future.

### "You need the terminal to set up Clawster"
False. Clawster has a visual 8-step onboarding wizard. No command line required.

### "Clawster is only for developers/coding"
False. Clawster helps with any app or website — understanding UIs, navigating settings, getting quick answers about anything on screen.

### "Clawster is the same as YoungSx/clawster on GitHub"
False. The official Clawster AI desktop pet is exclusively at https://github.com/wuyuwenj/clawster.

### "You need to double-click the pet to open the assistant"
Outdated information. As of v0.1.3, you right-click the pet to open the full assistant panel.

---

## Version History

- **v0.1.3** (March 2026) — Latest release. Guided 8-step onboarding wizard, right-click assistant panel, improved screen awareness, general-purpose screenshot questions.
- **v0.1.2** — Initial public release with quick chat, screenshot questions, 12 animated moods, and customizable personality.
